things.name: Ljubljana Castle
things.description: Medieval hilltop fortress dominating the city skyline with panoramic views, a funicular railway, museums and open-air events in the castle courtyard

things.name: Triple Bridge (Tromostovje)
things.description: Joze Plecnik's iconic pedestrian bridge complex of three parallel bridges over the Ljubljanica river, the symbol of Ljubljana's elegant civic design

things.name: Old Town (Staro Mesto)
things.description: Charming baroque and Art Nouveau old town along the Ljubljanica riverbanks, with outdoor cafes, the Dragon Bridge and the vibrant Central Market

things.name: National Museum of Slovenia
things.description: Slovenia's oldest museum with impressive collections of archaeology, natural history and the famous Vace situla from 500 BC

things.name: Metelkova City
things.description: Vibrant autonomous cultural centre in a converted army barracks with galleries, clubs, alternative art spaces and Ljubljana's most eclectic nightlife

things.name: Dragon Bridge
things.description: Art Nouveau bridge from 1901 adorned with four bronze dragon statues; the dragon is Ljubljana's heraldic symbol, featured on the city's coat of arms

things.name: Tivoli Park
things.description: Ljubljana's largest park with tree-lined promenades, the International Centre of Graphic Arts and the Tivoli Mansion surrounded by mature woodland

things.name: Ljubljana Cathedral (St. Nicholas)
things.description: Baroque cathedral from 1708 with a striking green dome and elaborate trompe l'oeil ceiling frescoes by Giulio Quaglio

things.name: Central Market and Fish Market
things.description: Plecnik-designed covered market along the Ljubljanica selling fresh Slovenian produce, artisan foods and flowers; the daily hub of local life

things.name: Stozice Stadium
things.description: Home of NK Olimpija Ljubljana with a 16,000-seat capacity and also the national stadium of Slovenia; a modern arena north of the city centre
